# cloudflareext_workers_observability_destination (Resource)

Manages a Cloudflare Workers Observability destination.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "cloudflareext_workers_observability_destination" "grafana_traces" {
  name            = "grafana-traces"
  enabled         = true
  type            = "logpush"
  url             = "https://otlp-gateway-prod-us-central-0.grafana.net/otlp/v1/traces"
  logpush_dataset = "opentelemetry_traces"

  headers_wo = {
    Authorization = "Basic example"
  }

  headers_wo_version = "1"
}

resource "cloudflareext_workers_observability_destination" "grafana_traces_stateful_headers" {
  name            = "grafana-traces-stateful"
  enabled         = true
  type            = "logpush"
  url             = "https://otlp-gateway-prod-us-central-0.grafana.net/otlp/v1/traces"
  logpush_dataset = "opentelemetry_traces"

  headers = {
    Authorization = "Basic example"
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `enabled` (Boolean) Whether the destination is enabled.
- `logpush_dataset` (String) The OpenTelemetry dataset for this destination.
- `name` (String) The destination name.
- `type` (String) The destination type. Currently only `logpush` is supported by Cloudflare.
- `url` (String) The OTLP endpoint URL.

### Optional

> **NOTE**: [Write-only arguments](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments) are supported in Terraform 1.11 and later.

- `headers` (Map of String, Sensitive) Custom headers sent to the OTLP endpoint (legacy). These values may contain authentication credentials and are stored in Terraform state. On Terraform 1.11+, use `headers_wo` instead.
- `headers_wo` (Map of String, [Write-only](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments)) Custom headers sent to the OTLP endpoint (write-only). These values are never stored in Terraform state. Requires Terraform 1.11 or later.
- `headers_wo_version` (String) A version number that should be incremented each time `headers_wo` changes. Since `headers_wo` is write-only and not stored in state, Terraform cannot detect when it changes.
- `skip_preflight_check` (Boolean) Whether to skip Cloudflare's destination preflight check during creation.

### Read-Only

- `destination_conf` (String) The generated Logpush destination configuration.
- `id` (String) The unique identifier of the destination. This is the destination slug.
- `logpush_job` (Number) The generated Logpush job identifier.
- `scripts` (List of String) Workers scripts that reference this destination.
- `slug` (String) The destination slug.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import cloudflareext_workers_observability_destination.grafana_traces grafana-traces
```
